summary: The five named patches mostly landed: browser-mode cookies are now applied lazily per origin before first navigation, probe data is persisted to `probe-snapshot.json`, BrightData responses are now binary-safe with upstream response headers preserved and bounded retries, the MPA probe path now filters dominant soft-200 clusters, and crawl parent/edge data now reaches both the snapshot and `pages.json`. I do not see the earlier review’s blocking failures still present in those exact areas. The remaining problems are integration gaps rather than hard blockers: `--via` still drops caller auth/UA,M

issues.description: `--via brightdata` still ignores caller-side auth/request identity for GETs. `runAudit()` only forwards `{ zone, country }` into `makeViaFetcher()`, so `--cookie`, `--user-agent`, and any future request headers are silently discarded on the managed-fetch path. That leaves auth-walled or header-sensitive sites unclonable when `--via` is selected, even though the CLI still accepts those options.
issues.suggested_fix: Thread request context into the via fetcher, e.g. `makeViaFetcher(via, { zone, country, cookie, userAgent })`, and have the provider request include those values in the provider-specific request shape or documented fallback behavior. At minimum, warn explicitly when `--via` is used with unsupported auth/header options.

issues.description: The crawl-graph plumbing is only partially integrated. `runAudit()` now passes `crawlPages`/`crawlEdges` into `buildAnatomy()`, but `buildAnatomy()` still destructures only `{ outRoot, publicDir, target, probe, detection }` and never consumes the new graph inputs. `sitepull_map` also regenerates anatomy without reloading `pages.json`, so the claimed graph-aware anatomy regeneration is still a no-`
issues.suggested_fix: Extend `buildAnatomy()` to accept and use `crawlPages`/`crawlEdges`, and have `sitepull_map` reload `pages.json` (or the crawl section from `probe-snapshot.json`) before regenerating anatomy so reruns preserve route topology.

issues.description: Anatomy chain generation is schema-drifted from probe output. `buildChains()` reads `ep.url` and `ep.contentType`, but `probeEndpoints()` stores probe records as `{ path, status, len, ct }`. As a result, anatomy chains fall back to `external` for host labeling and `unknown` for content type, even after the new snapshot reload path works.
issues.suggested_fix: Normalize the probe schema in one place. Either store `{ url, contentType }` during probing, or update anatomy generation to derive the full URL from `target + ep.path` and read `ep.ct` instead of `ep.contentType`.

issues.description: The new persisted edge graph has no deduplication. Repeated nav/footer links on large MPAs will emit the same `{ from, to, kind }` edge many times, inflating `pages.json` and `probe-snapshot.json` without adding information.
issues.suggested_fix: Track a `Set` of serialized edges during crawl and only persist unique edges.

strengths: The browser cookie fix is real: cookies are now bound lazily to the first navigated origin before either page or API requests run.
The probe sidecar is now written by `runAudit()`, and `sitepull_map` does reload probe/fuzz data from it instead of regenerating empty chains by default.
The BrightData fetcher now uses `arrayBuffer()` and slices buffers correctly, which fixes the earlier binary corruption path for vendored non-text assets.
The new MPA shell heuristic materially improves false-positive handling for soft-200/WAF challenge pages by clustering dominant response signatures.
Crawl parent/edge data now survives the run and is emitted into sibling `pages.json`, which is a useful compatibility layer for downstream consumers.
